match_nodes.description: Telecommunications Business Act — Japan's secrecy-of-communications guarantee for carriers/ISPs

data.notes: Act No. 86 of 1984, heavily amended (most recently for 2022/2023 cookie-consent-style amendments). Large act (200+ articles): Chapter I General Provisions (Art. 1-5, includes Art. 4 secrecy-of-communications guarantee and Art. 3 no-censorship provision) and Chapter II Telecommunications Business (Art. 6-40+, registration and business conduct), plus further chapters on facilities, dispute resolution, penalties. Article 4's secrecy guarantee is the primary legal constraint on carriers/ISPs inspecting, retaining, or disclosing traffic content or metadata — directly relevant to lawful-intercept limits, deep packet inspection, and threat-monitoring programs, and explicitly implicated by the Active Cyber Defense Act's communications-monitoring provisions (secid:regulation/cyber.go.jp/basic-act-cybersecurity).
